sections.paragraph:
- Original: συνάγω

- Transliteration: sunago

- Phonetic: soon-ag'-o

- Definition:

1. to gather together, to gather

a. to draw together, collect

1. of fishes

2. of a net in which they are caught

2. to bring together, assemble, collect

a. to join together, join in one (those previously separated)

b. to gather together by convoking

c. to be gathered i.e. come together, gather, meet

3. to lead with one's self

a. into one's home, i.e. to receive hospitably, to entertain

- Origin: from G4862 and G71

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4862 and G71; to lead together that is collect or convene; specifically to entertain (hospitably): - + accompany assemble (selves together) bestow come together gather (selves together up together) lead into resort take in.
